name: Rockpool Coffin Bay Scallops with Braised Capsicum & Toma
recipeInstructions:
1. Blanch, peel, and dice 2 capsicums and 4 tomatoes.
2. Finely dice 2 cloves of garlic.
3. Wash and dry 12 basil leaves, 12 tarragon fronds, and 24 sprigs of continental parsley.
4. In a little olive oil, pan-fry the capsicums and garlic for a minute or two.
5. Add the tomatoes to the pan, season, and cook for another minute.
6. Serve the mixture onto plates.
7. Put a little more olive oil in the pan and sauté the Coffin Bay scallops.
8. Scatter the herbs (basil, tarragon, and parsley) into the pan to crisp and become translucent.
9. Scatter the cooked scallops onto the plates.
10. Deglaze the pan with lemon juice, virgin olive oil, and pepper.
11. Pour the deglazed sauce over the servings.
Posted by Stephen Ceideburg
From an article by Meryl Constance in The Sydney Morning Herald, 6/15/93.
Courtesy Mark Herron.
